title: HeadObject, GetObject, Conditions, Ranges, and Cleanup · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs headobject, getobject, conditions, ranges, and cleanup from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded S3 download decision and cleanup flow after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose HEAD, GET, conditional GET, or a metadata database lookup without doubling requests or trusting stale authority.
Check response and metadata, own and close the body, propagate cancellation, handle partial reads, and distinguish transport from caller writes.
Map application validators and preconditions to S3 requests and preserve 304, 412, not-found, and version-specific meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Head Vs Get
steps.instruction:

content.text: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 3241 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned conditional range and prove S3 response-body cleanup; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose HEAD, GET, conditional GET, or a metadata database lookup without doubling requests or trusting stale authority.

AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify missing key, missing version, delete marker, denied access, archive restore state, throttling, cancellation, and partial downstream response.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Download Error Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Returning 404 for every S3 failure prevents information disclosure without operational cost.
